verdict: Worth doing, high-confidence YES — this is the single cheapest, highest-leverage v1.0 move and it is a hard prerequisite for delete-dead-code. The evidence is unusually clean: tests reach every plumbing class via deep `../src/runtime/*.js` paths (never via the barrel), and the only documented/exampled adopter surface is exactly NodeBridge/PyodideBridge/HttpBridge + setRuntimeBridge + the arrow codec registration helpers + defineConfig/generate + the error classes + config/type exports. ProcessIO, HttpIO, PyodideIO, WorkerPool, PooledTransport, BridgeProtocol, BoundedContext, SafeCodec, Transport guards and ~15 validators appear in ZERO docs and have ZERO bare-`'tywrap'` consumers — they are pure semver liability. I scope DOWN from the review on one point: rather than create a new `tywrap/internal` subpath (which just re-freezes the same plumbing under a different name and invites the same masking), the plumbing should simply NOT be exported from any published entrypoint. The deep `src/runtime/*.js` paths tests use are not part of `package.json#exports`, so they are already non-public; keeping the classes file-level `export`ed for tests is fine and does not constitute a published contract. The ONLY thing worth a deliberate escape hatch is `SafeCodec`/`CodecOptions` (advanced users wiring a custom bridge) and the Transport interface contract — park those behind `./runtime` rather than the root, not `./internal`.
verifiedSignals: REVIEW.md B4 (index.ts:13-63 over-broad barrel): CONFIRMED — src/index.ts:36-42 exports ProcessIO/HttpIO/PyodideIO/WorkerPool/PooledTransport and :45-63 exports ~16 validators; all are internal plumbing.

SIGNAL-DIGEST deadcode-blind claim: CONFIRMED the mechanism — barrel re-exports make modules statically reachable. Proven dynamically: validators isFiniteNumber/isPositiveNumber/isNonNegativeNumber/isNonEmptyString/assertNonNegative/assertArray/assertObject/assertNoSpecialFloats/sanitizeForFilename/containsPathTraversal have NO src caller other than their def + the index.ts barrel (grep across src excl tests).

Real adopter contract = examples/living-app/src/index.ts:5-7: imports NodeBridge from 'tywrap/node', setRuntimeBridge from 'tywrap/runtime', autoRegisterArrowDecoder+clearArrowDecoder from 'tywrap'. Nothing else.

Tests never consume the barrel for plumbing: test/transport.test.ts:21-23, test/worker-pool.test.ts:13, test/bridge-protocol.test.ts:14-15, test/safe-codec.test.ts:9, test/bounded-context.test.ts:13, test/validators.test.ts:31, test/arch-stories.test.ts:35 ALL import via '../src/runtime/*.js' deep paths. Only bare-'tywrap' test consumer is defineConfig (test/config_loading.test.ts:16).

RuntimeBridge (src/index.ts:68, deprecated) has ZERO importers other than the barrel itself — `rg RuntimeBridge` finds only src/index.ts:68 importing from runtime/base.js; getRuntimeBridge/setRuntimeBridge are a SEPARATE registry in src/runtime/index.ts:9-24.
BridgeProtocol/BoundedContext/SafeCodec/ProcessIO/WorkerPool/PooledTransport appear in 0 doc files (rg across docs/).

decodeValue (src/utils/codec.ts) only consumed by dead code: src/utils/bundle-optimizer.ts and src/runtime/bridge-core.ts — both on the delete-dead-code list, so it is publicly-exported-only once they are gone.

package.json exports map (lines 8-33) currently exposes ., ./node, ./pyodide, ./http, ./runtime, ./dev; ./runtime (src/runtime/index.ts) currently exports ONLY the 3 registry fns — docs uniformly import setRuntimeBridge from 'tywrap/runtime' (10+ doc hits).
falsePositives: REVIEW.md B4 suggests moving plumbing behind 'tywrap/internal'. REFUTED as the right shape: a published ./internal subpath re-freezes the same modules into the exports map and re-creates the exact static-reachability masking that blinds deadcode analysis. The deep src/runtime/*.js paths tests already use are NOT in package.json#exports, so they are non-public today; no ./internal entrypoint is needed. Source: package.json:8-33 lists no ./internal, yet every plumbing test imports fine via deep paths.

Implicit assumption that the plumbing classes might have hidden internal consumers needing the barrel: REFUTED — every src consumer imports them via relative './runtime/*.js' paths, never via 'tywrap' (the barrel is leaf, imported by no other src file). Removing barrel lines changes zero internal resolution.

SIGNAL-DIGEST lists memory-profiler.ts:491 require-in-ESM as the lone true positive 'but dead': not a barrel concern, but it CONFIRMS bundle-optimizer/bridge-core are dead, which is why decodeValue becomes export-only — consistent with treating decodeValue as removable once delete-dead-code lands.
proposal:
Target v1.0 published surface across the exports map:

ROOT 'tywrap' (src/index.ts) — the codegen + types + shared-vocabulary entrypoint:
- defineConfig, resolveConfig (config/index.ts) + types TywrapConfig, ResolvedTywrapConfig
- generate + types GenerateFailure, GenerateResult, GenerateRunOptions (tywrap.ts). Demote tywrap() factory per G5 (keep exported but document generate as primary; out of scope here).
- The 6 error classes: BridgeError, BridgeCodecError, BridgeProtocolError, BridgeTimeoutError, BridgeDisposedError, BridgeExecutionError (errors.ts) — adopters catch these.
- Arrow codec registration: autoRegisterArrowDecoder, registerArrowDecoder, clearArrowDecoder, decodeValue, decodeValueAsync (utils/codec.ts) — used by living-app + 4-6 docs.
- Runtime detection: detectRuntime, isNodejs, isDeno, isBun, isBrowser (utils/runtime.ts) — small, documented, harmless DX.
- Public IR/config TYPE exports from types/index.ts (the PythonModule/PythonType/*Config family) — these ARE the type-safe contract for config authors; keep as type-only.
- VERSION (sourced from package.json per Quick Win 3, not hardcoded).
- type Disposable + isDisposable/safeDispose/disposeAll — KEEP (small, generated-wrapper-adjacent lifecycle helpers, used in dev.ts public API). Borderline; acceptable to keep at root.
- Default export = generate (or tywrap factory) — keep one.

'tywrap/node', 'tywrap/pyodide', 'tywrap/http' — UNCHANGED: each exposes its Bridge class + Options interface (NodeBridge/NodeBridgeOptions etc). This is the canonical bridge-import path (living-app uses tywrap/node).

'tywrap/runtime' (src/runtime/index.ts) — the generated-wrapper + advanced-bridge contract:
- KEEP getRuntimeBridge, setRuntimeBridge, clearRuntimeBridge (generated code imports getRuntimeBridge from here; docs import setRuntimeBridge from here).
- MOVE the genuinely-advanced extension contract here (re-export from runtime/index.ts): type Transport, TransportOptions, ProtocolMessage, ProtocolResponse, PROTOCOL_ID, isTransport, isProtocolMessage, isProtocolResponse, SafeCodec + CodecOptions. Rationale: someone authoring a custom Transport/bridge legitimately needs these; they do NOT belong at root. This is the ONE deliberate escape hatch — narrow and named.

'tywrap/dev' — UNCHANGED.

NOT EXPORTED from any published entrypoint (kept as plain file-level exports for tests via deep src paths, which are not in exports map): ProcessIO/ProcessIOOptions, HttpIO/HttpIOOptions, PyodideIO/PyodideIOOptions, WorkerPool/WorkerPoolOptions/PooledWorker, PooledTransport/PooledTransportOptions, BridgeProtocol/BridgeProtocolOptions, BoundedContext/ContextState/ExecuteOptions, ValidationError, and all ~16 validators (isFiniteNumber, isPositiveNumber, isNonNegativeNumber, isNonEmptyString, isPlainObject, assertFiniteNumber, assertPositive, assertNonNegative, assertString, assertNonEmptyString, assertArray, assertObject, containsSpecialFloat, assertNoSpecialFloats, sanitizeForFilename, containsPathTraversal).

DELETED OUTRIGHT: RuntimeBridge (deprecated re-export at index.ts:68, zero consumers) — drop the export; the base.ts class itself is handled by delete-dead-code.

Net result: root barrel shrinks from ~50 runtime/validator exports to a curated ~20-symbol surface; the plumbing modules become statically unreachable from any exports-map entrypoint, which is exactly what un-blinds the deadcode scan and makes delete-dead-code provable.
breakingChanges: REMOVED from 'tywrap' root: ProcessIO, ProcessIOOptions (was index.ts:36).
REMOVED from 'tywrap' root: WorkerPool, WorkerPoolOptions, PooledWorker (index.ts:40).
REMOVED from 'tywrap' root: BoundedContext, ContextState, ExecuteOptions (index.ts:13-17).

REMOVED from 'tywrap' root: ValidationError and all ~16 validators isFiniteNumber/isPositiveNumber/isNonNegativeNumber/isNonEmptyString/isPlainObject/assertFiniteNumber/assertPositive/assertNonNegative/assertString/assertNonEmptyString/assertArray/assertObject/containsSpecialFloat/assertNoSpecialFloats/sanitizeForFilename/containsPathTraversal (index.ts:45-63).
DELETED entirely (no replacement): RuntimeBridge deprecated re-export (index.ts:68).
MOVED root -> 'tywrap/runtime': Transport, TransportOptions, ProtocolMessage, ProtocolResponse, PROTOCOL_ID, isTransport, isProtocolMessage, isProtocolResponse (was index.ts:22-34).

No change to tywrap/node, tywrap/pyodide, tywrap/http, tywrap/dev. No change to the exports MAP keys (still ., ./node, ./pyodide, ./http, ./runtime, ./dev) — only the symbols inside . and ./runtime change.

Migration: custom-bridge authors switch `import { Transport, SafeCodec } from 'tywrap'` to `from 'tywrap/runtime'`. Anyone who imported a validator from 'tywrap' must inline it (these were never documented). RuntimeBridge users were already deprecated -> use NodeBridge/PyodideBridge/HttpBridge.
verificationPlan: 1) Behavior-preserving for the codebase: no test imports plumbing/validators via the bare 'tywrap' specifier (proven: only defineConfig does), and no src file imports the barrel — so `npm run build` + `npm test` + `npm run test:types` must pass unchanged with zero edits to test files. 2) Surface snapshot: add a tiny `.api-surface` characterization test that imports * from 'tywrap' and 'tywrap/runtime' and asserts the exact exported key set (locks v1.0 contract; doubles as the migration-guide source of truth). 3) Un-blinding proof: after trimming, re-run ts-fx deadcode (`/tmp/fx-tywrap` pipeline) and confirm it now flags the dead modules (parallel-processor, bundle-optimizer, memory-profiler, bridge-core, protocol, optimized-node) that were previously masked — this is the gate proving the ordering dependency is satisfied. 4) Consumer smoke: `npm run example:living-app:smoke` must pass unchanged (its only root imports are autoRegisterArrowDecoder/clearArrowDecoder, both retained). 5) `npm run check:node`/`check:deno`/`check:bun` to confirm the trimmed entrypoints still resolve.
steps: Add a characterization test test/api-surface.test.ts that imports namespace * from 'tywrap' and from 'tywrap/runtime' (build first) and asserts the CURRENT exported-key sets, to capture the pre-refactor baseline for the migration guide.

Edit src/index.ts: delete the plumbing exports — lines 13-17 (BoundedContext+types), 18-19 (BridgeProtocol), 36-42 (ProcessIO/HttpIO/PyodideIO/WorkerPool/PooledTransport), 45-63 (ValidationError + all validators), and 68 (RuntimeBridge deprecated re-export). Keep the file-level `export` keywords on the source modules themselves (process-io.ts etc.) so tests' deep imports still resolve.
Edit src/index.ts: REMOVE SafeCodec/CodecOptions (line 21) and the Transport block (lines 22-34: types + PROTOCOL_ID + guards) from root.

Edit src/runtime/index.ts: ADD re-exports of the advanced escape-hatch surface — `export { SafeCodec, type CodecOptions } from './safe-codec.js';` and `export { PROTOCOL_ID, isTransport, isProtocolMessage, isProtocolResponse, type Transport, type TransportOptions, type ProtocolMessage, type ProtocolResponse } from './transport.js';` alongside the existing registry fns.

Confirm root keeps: defineConfig/resolveConfig + config types, generate + Generate* types, the 6 Bridge*Error classes, codec helpers (autoRegisterArrowDecoder/registerArrowDecoder/clearArrowDecoder/decodeValue/decodeValueAsync), runtime detection fns, the types/index.ts type family, Disposable+isDisposable/safeDispose/disposeAll, VERSION (sourced from package.json per Quick Win 3), and the default export.
Update the api-surface characterization test to the NEW expected key sets; this diff IS the migration guide payload.
Run npm run build && npm run typecheck && npm test && npm run test:types && npm run check:node — expect green with no test-file edits.
Run npm run example:living-app:smoke to confirm the canonical adopter path is intact.
Re-run the ts-fx deadcode scan and confirm the previously-masked dead modules now surface; hand off to delete-dead-code.

Write docs/guide/migration.md entries enumerating every moved/removed symbol (mirrors breakingChanges); update docs/reference/api/index.md to document tywrap/runtime as the advanced/custom-bridge entrypoint (Transport + SafeCodec).
